id: NOMAD - issues - Rendering an ActivityStep [1315690743704059905] (2024-10-01 to 2024-12-31)|q1-a2
thread_id: NOMAD - issues - Rendering an ActivityStep [1315690743704059905] (2024-10-01 to 2024-12-31)
title: Rendering an ActivityStep
question: Could be again that they are not `Schema`? Or is it already `Schema `or `EntryData` in an upper level?
proposed_answer: Yes, NeXus is a Schema What is put into data This Schema allows having NeXusMeasurements (e.g. Mpes) which are Measurements with an extra normalisation that sets the method used. The NexusMeasurements have Entries which are ActivitySteps. Note that I am normalising our Measurements so that `NexusMeasurement.steps` (inherited from `Measurement.steps`) hold the different ActivitySteps. In this particular case, there are 2 of them, both are of type `Mpes.ENTRY` which is subclass of `Entry` that is subclass of `ActivityStep` (as shown on the first screenshot). Without this normalisation, they render properly and show the ActivityStep related features, like `step name`: Here, there are no ActivityStep-s registered below `steps`, but as soon as I add a specialised one, I got the error above. Talking to Andrea, we have found some working examples os steps e.g. as tey are used by Experiment BaseSection and its subclasses: For https://github.com/PDI-Berlin/pdi-nomad-plugin/blob/d68e1c408ec6c6ffdfdc6c7b5703f093e9cbac6b/src/pdi_nomad_plugin/mbe/processes.py#L1185, I have some notes Activity.steps are defined as a Subsection (of ActivityStep-s) https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/ …

question: is there a plugin for the schema :)?
proposed_answer: Hi again @Micha, sorry for the delay. Let me try to give an overview here of how our current implementation works: **Current State** Currently in NOMAD, the core of the simulation schema is stored in [runschema](https://github.com/nomad-coe/nomad-schema-plugin-run) - In the archive, the location is archive.run instead of archive.data (like for experiments) - Within this section, you may store *either* a single system, method, calculation (output) or multiples of each Simulation workflows in NOMAD are stored according to [nomad-schema-plugin-simulation-workflow](https://github.com/nomad-coe/nomad-schema-plugin-simulation-workflow) - In the archive, the location is archive.workflow2 We consider a workflow to be any protocol which takes you between instances of the system. For example, a geometry optimization is a workflow. A molecular dynamics simulation is also a workflow. These types of standard workflows are often executed within a single code. For that reason, we store them within a single entry. More generally though, you can construct simulation workflows arbitrarily. These "custom" workflows can arbitrarily link together simulations (whether they were performed all at once or …

title: discussion
question: is there a docker container which makes it possible to run the nomad python package on client side on windows? did someone thought about this? A user of mine deleted all of his uploads. I have backups including mongo dumps elastic etc. what is the best way to restore a selected set of uploads?
proposed_answer: Sorry for the late reply, I just saw this. I have no actual experience with this. Especially be careful with the `mongorestore` . Do dry runs and double check what i am writing here. **1. restore the mongo upload and entry documents.** Theoretically you should be able to use `mongorestore` (see our [docs](<https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/staging/docs/howto/oasis/admin.html#backups>) and the mongo [docs](<https://www.mongodb.com/docs/database-tools/mongorestore/>)) on the whole backup as `mongorestore` supposidly on restores objects that are not already present (e.g. deleted). But I would really test this first; make sure you do not activate `--drop` (drops all collections before restoring them); use `--dryRun`. **2. put the files back** If you know the upload id, you could just put the right directories back. Otherwise, you have to look into rsync or something to put the right files back. You can also use the restored mongo uploads and try to use some mongoquery involving the user id or something. I guess, you are familier with the file layout. All uploads live in `.volumes/fs/[public|staging]/<first-letter-of-id>/<upload-id>` . You only need to put back those `<upload-id>` dirs that …

title: general-comp
question: Should the *.traj files from ASE be supported? to generate entries with structures and energies? It seems NOMAD somehow recognizes the traj files and creates entries, but they are empty. Should I open an issue in the electronic parser github?
proposed_answer: Hi Julia, It seems there is a parser for that (ASAP, https://asap3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/): https://github.com/nomad-coe/atomistic-parsers/tree/develop/atomisticparsers/asap, but it is a bit empty. In the former structure of parsers, this will fall in the category of "atomistic", instead of "electronic", but I don't think this is relevant anymore. With the new plugins structure (https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-plugin-template), new schema (https://github.com/nomad-coe/nomad-simulations), and seeing that the parser is not that complicated, I would encourage: 1. Create a new plugin repo `nomad_parser_asap` 2. Use the new schema whenever possible, and extend it when 3. Add this plugin to the nomad when is ready (https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/merge_requests/1931). In case the parser already exists in a legacy repo ("electronic-parsers", "atomistic-parsers"...), you just need to delete the `/nomad/config/defaults.yaml` lines corresponding to this old parser. Now, this is a trajectories case, so probably the schema is not there yet. I am sure @Joseph Rudzinski and @Bernadette Mohr have a much better idea of how to handle point 2. But if the *.traj files are …

question: I guess then it is that way. I couldn't get netcdf4 to install when I did a pip install nomad. There is some odd error. I couldn't test the import. Do you know if we can choose [all] somehow from package.find?
proposed_answer: > Do you know if we can choose [all] somehow from package.find? I don't think that will work because the plugins are their own packages and not actually submodules of pynxtools. Essentially, they are their own packages that should be installed additionally. The best would be to install pynxtools[all] as dependency in nomad, as suggested by Florian in this MR: https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/merge_requests/1629. Then, one could even install just one of the plugins in your local OASIS by overwriting in the pyproject.toml, e.g. pynxtools[mpes]. However, since this MR is currently breaking the tests and in general not working, the only workaround that I can think of is to add a manual install of pynxtools to the Dockerfile as we had it before: ``` WORKDIR /install/dependencies/parsers/nexus RUN pip install -r requirements.txt RUN pip install --no-deps ".[all]" WORKDIR /install ``` This is why I was asking if this manual installation is still done somewhere.

question: Is there any specific context in which you would like to make the CIF files searchable? The materials card in the overview page gets the data from `results.material` and `results.material.topology`. Basically, you could have a schema with a `structure_file` quantity that takes the CIF file(s) and populates this. We do something along this line in an experimental plugin that we are developing for XRD analysis. I think we should have support for a base section that provides this functionality out of the box. @Sarthak Kapoor, @Hampus Näsström, @Lauri Himanen, any thoughts?
proposed_answer: @ron_do Hi, pls feel free to link your project. It'll provide a lot of useful context to identify the issue. Most importantly is how you connected the CIF file to the schema. Here are the possibilities that I'd try out, in order: 1. It sounds like you're parsing the CIF file into `run.system`. In that case, this isn't really a calculation, hence why populating `results` fails. This is what @Joseph Rudzinski was pointing out. 2. If you want to upload structure files without any calculation attached, it'd be better to provide it as its own entry type. Then we can use `data`, which bypasses `results`, for visualization. 3. Or we don't use the native NOMAD visualizer and instead explore the NORTH tools.

question: Do we have already a standard NOMAD way for publishing optical spectroscopy data? How does this compare?
proposed_answer: Hey @Julia Schumann, very interesting. I will take a closer look next week. From the computational side, I thought a lot about this. Doing simpler data modeling and Python implementation, I came up with the concept of `SpectralProfile`. This is an abstract class which has defined the intensities as an array and some validations to check that these are defined positive numbers. This is then used to inherit in `DOS` and in `AbsorptionSpectrum`, which in turns is inherited in `XASSpectrum`. This is for data that we already have in the database. For UV-Vis, I'll argue that then it will be about doing the same than with XAS. And similarly for IR, Raman... Now, in terms of material properties, I think the question would be to define the AbsorptionCoefficient and possibly connected it with SpectralProfile, but this is not done yet and I guess people can have other ideas.

title: User ID for new oasis
question: Ok, so on my server there is a docker group of which I'm a user. ``` frijtersch@app-nomad01:~/nomad/tno-solar-nomad-image$ getent group | grep docker docker:x:986:frijtersch frijtersch@app-nomad01:~/nomad/tno-solar-nomad-image$ id uid=110867669(frijtersch) ``` In this case I would have to run`chown -R 110867669:986 .volumes` and in the docker-compose file set `users: 110867669:986` for the north config?
proposed_answer: No, `docker-compose.yaml`should read `users: 1000:986`. I can't say I understand it completely, but UID 1000 is hardcoded by nomad and needs to be the same inside the docker containers and the system. `sudo chown -R 1000 .volumes` should suffice. Note that there are security considerations with the docker group: https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/linux-postinstall/ > The docker group grants root-level privileges to the user.

question: I have rebased.. I skipped wherever required.. Could you please check if I have done it correctly?
proposed_answer: It somehow still has a different set of definitions compared to master. This is not intentional, right? Rebasing is tricky when you have submodules. If you are fine to use the definitions at master, can you run from the command line ``` git submodule sync --recursive git submodule update --init --recursive --remote --jobs=4 git submodule foreach --recursive 'git fetch --tags' cd src/pynxtools/definitions printf "$(git describe --dirty --tags --long --abbrev=8 --match '*[0-9]*')" > ../nexus-version.txt ``` That should set the definitions to the latest, which is also what we have in the master branch Also, you'll need to add owlready2, fastapi, and pygit2 to the pyproject.toml file under dependencies See this CI/CD workflow failing: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/pynxtools/actions/runs/16651056418/job/47123603244?pr=680

question: I am trying to do something similar and agree with @Klaus Götz , the documentation should be improved. Do we have a minimal example somewhere? Do I need to set `self.creates_children = True` in the parser class that is inheriting from MatchingParser? And how do I need to structure the child_archives dict?
proposed_answer: yes, it should be set to the instance of `MatchingParser`, If is_mainifile returns a list of strings, then `child_archives` will be a key: archive dict. you can access this in your parse function, i.e. `def parse(self, mainfile, archive, logger, child_archives):` I have created an issue to add this in the docs here: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-docs/issues/17

title: general-comp
question: Hi everyone! This is perhaps a ridiculously simple question, but I am new to this area and to NOMAD. What is the simplest way to download all of the data from NOMAD (sync it locally) such that one can do simple things such as making a Venn-diagram of all of the different classes of materials that exist in NOMAD?
proposed_answer: Hi @chaxor ! Thanks for posting, any questions are welcome 😁 You can make an API call to query the NOMAD repository for certain information from certain entries or from all entries, without downloading the data (there are 112 TBs of data on NOMAD!). However, you may not even need to do this, depending on what you mean by "different classes of materials". If you go to the Calculations APP (https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/gui/search/calculations) you will already find characterizations like "space group" and "crystal system" in the widgets on display by default. You can find further characterizations by clicking on the filter bars under `Materials` on the left hand side. NOMAD already provides many classifications of the material for you without you having to analyze the data yourself. If you share more specifically what kind of classification you are trying to achieve, we can direct you on the best route to get there 🤓

question: That sounds great! Nothing super urgent on my side but I definitely see some use cases where it could be handy 🙂 Do you want me to put this in a Gitlab issue or do you prefer to do it?
proposed_answer: I'm actually wrong about the first point: you can already do negation through the API. You can see an example in our API endpoint docs (`/entries/query`). You just need to wrap things in a dictionary with the `not` key. So what we could do is to introduce a search bar syntax for easily doing such queries. An easy step would be simply to have the `!=` operator, but in the long run we will probably want to negate some bigger logical groups (e.g. `!(C or H)`). So the overarching Gitlab issue should be about extending the search bar syntax. We just migrated the basic search functionality to the new GUI, and this would be a good point to extend the search bar functionality as well. I created an issue in the `nomad-gui` project: https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-gui/-/issues/72

question: @ahmed ilyas I am a bit confused about this So, in terms of plugin versioning, for the case that my plugin is included in the pyproject.toml of the FAIRmat distro-dev with <my_plugin>>=x.x.x, my understanding is that: - once I make a release of my plugin, the dev deployment will be updated accordingly at midnight. Correct? - Does this also apply eventually to staging and production when the versions are bumped up? Now, based on what you said above, it appears that plugin dependencies are dealt with differently: - So, if I update a plugin dependency, I need to also change the requirements file …
proposed_answer: > once I make a release of my plugin, the dev deployment will be updated accordingly at midnight. Correct? This is only true for nomad-lab, only nomad-lab package will be updated every night. Test oasis is slightly different, some of the plugins there depends on the main branch instead of a release so they also get updated, but it's test oasis so it's a different deployment. > Does this also apply eventually to staging and production when the versions are bumped up? Only nomad-lab gets updated. So for the main deployments (develop, staging, test, prod) the plugins will need to be updated manually. > So, if I update a plugin dependency, I need to also change the requirements file on nomad-FAIR? We only have the plugins in nomad-FAIR to run the test suite, there's a related issue for this (https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/issues/2255). Once those tests are removed, we can remove the `default_plugins.txt` entirely. So the list on nomad-FAIR doesn't affect any deployments.

question: Hi everyone! How can we determine which plugin to use when reading a file and how do we navigate graphs on NOMAD?
proposed_answer: HI Astou, I'll try to provide some answers for the two questions: 1. What do you mean by "reading a file"? Do you mean when you upload a file to NOMAD? What parser gets called is decide by the matching criteria. The simplest criteria is to match on either the file name/ending or the mime type of the file using the `MatchingParser` class. You can find more information about this in the docs here: https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/docs/explanation/processing.html#matching 2. Not sure exactly what you mean by graphs. NOMAD doesn't have a graph backend but we do have an option of finding the back references between entries. These are resolved in the GUI so to navigate in a graphical sense you can follow the references where they are place in the data section and the back references can all be found on the overview page. As far as I know we have no API endpoint for querying across multiple references, this would have to be done on the client side. Maybe if you provide some more information on what you want to do I could provide more information.

question: can you show me what is inside of your `response` variable?
proposed_answer: It's works now. Though I have a different question now. See the code below. ``` import os upload_ids = [] for i in range(1, 11): file_dir = "../tars" file_name = f"{i:04d}.tar" file_path = os.path.join(file_dir, file_name) print(file_path) upload_id = upload_to_NOMAD(nomad_url, token, file_path ) metadata = { "metadata": { "datasets": dataset_id, }, } edit_upload_metadata(nomad_url, token, upload_id, metadata) if upload_id: upload_ids.append(upload_id) print(upload_ids) #publish_upload(nomad_url, token, upload_id) ``` I want to upload ~1000 tar files. Each `0001.tar` contains a `workflow.archive.yaml`, a subdirectory and few files. Seems like I cannot publish inside the upload loop. Therefore, I have stored `upload_id` in a variable and running another loop for publishing. Since, I can only have 10 unpublished uploads, what should I do to speedup the process? question: Anyone else having trouble with setup_dev_env.sh? I run into a conflict: ```sh ERROR: Cannot install -r default_plugins.txt (line 7) and nomad-schema-plugin-run 1.0 (from git+https://github.com/nomad-coe/nomad-schema-plugin-run.git@169029274e282a54df3292f6aa0d7bfbeb95ef9c) because these package versions have conflicting dependencies. The conflict is caused by: The user requested nomad-schema-plugin-run 1.0 (from git+https://github.com/nomad-coe/nomad-schema-plugin-run.git@169029274e282a54df3292f6aa0d7bfbeb95ef9c) nomad-schema-plugin-simulation-workflow 1.0 depends on nomad-schema-plugin-run 1. …
proposed_answer: Yeah in CI we use `uv pip` instead of standard pip for this. In `uv` the `@develop` will resolve to the actual commit SHA which is equal to what we have in requirements file so there's no conflict. But in `setup_dev_env.sh` pip is used, which is presumably trying to compare `@develop` with a sha and it doesn't match. I think we might have to update the dependency in workflow plugin here Or we can use `uv pip` in setup_dev_env.sh too

When comparing to NIAC main, the changes on the files you mentioned above are much smaller. These fall into three categories: - changed the copyright starting date for files that we recently merged into NIAC main, but where we had (unintentionally) set all starting dates to 2014. Like `NXactivity`, `NXfabrication`, etc. We should probably make a PR to NIAC where we make these starting dates correct as well. For this, I used the mentioned script by the way. - there are some files where we have additional modifications which we have not proposed or are not yet merged to NIAC, e.g. `NXactuator` has `NXpid_controller`. title: discussion
question: Hi! We don't have explicit data types for unsigned numbers in the metainfo, but storing unsigned values (`np.uint32`) to an Quantity with type='int32' should work. Maybe you give a minimal example of where the type checks fail? Is it happening at the definition level (in your schema) or at the parsing level (when assigning values)?
proposed_answer: The type check fails during parsing. Calling `MDParser.parse_trajectory` with arrays of type `uint32`: ``` self.parse_trajectory_step(particles_dict, simulation) ``` ``` File "/home/bmohr/software/nomad-parser-gsd/.pyenv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/nomad/metainfo/metainfo.py", line 3056, in __set__ value = self.type.normalize(value, section=obj) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/home/bmohr/software/nomad-parser-gsd/.pyenv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/nomad/metainfo/data_type.py", line 323, in normalize raise ValueError(f'Cannot convert {value} to {self._dtype}.') ValueError: Cannot convert [[44481 44482] [44482 44483] [44483 44484] ... [68477 68478] [68478 68479] [68479 68480]] to <class 'numpy.int32'>. ``` Type casting at parser level absolutely works, I just wanted to make sure there isn't some accepted protocol how we deal with similar cases.

question: do you talk about some instrument like a table-top hand crafted one, such as the one built for non linear spectroscopy experiments and other things that is hard to structure because non standard? You may want to check how deep in structuring you want to go and which is the level of structuring needed for you to consume that data afterwards. This is very important. Maybe a flat class where you list your sensors timeseries or other arrays in enough for you. Base classes approach is useful on the other hand, e.g. "divide et impera" approach. What I mean is that maybe your setup is not standard bu …
proposed_answer: Here’s my data structure, which is intended to handle a wide range of settings. As you can see, it covers numerous configurations, some of which are specific to individual instruments. This creates a broad spectrum of possibilities. My question is: if a dynamic approach isn't feasible, what would be the best solution? Should I create a comprehensive set of sections that covers all possible instrument configurations? Or would it make more sense to align with NeXus development? On that note, I'm currently working with Seb5G, who is in discussions with Sandor about handling multidimensional arrays. We would really appreciate the chance to meet with you to discuss possible solutions and our role in this project. Thank you.

question: @ChemaP This is what I do not understand at the moment It seems that something which is part of the definition (?, I mean part of the annotation) is not necessarily stored in the archive (only something which is a quantity is). To make things even more complicated: Is there a programmatic way for a schema to know which quantities have units assigned to them? So when I load something from a file and use something like ```for key in config_data['database']: setattr(config.database, key, config_data['database'][key])``` Is there a way so that the normalize method would then be able to check if al …
proposed_answer: > It seems that something which is part of the definition (?, I mean part of the annotation) is not necessarily stored in the archive (only something which is a quantity is). Okay, I think I understand your question better now. There are actually 2 approaches, let me iterate over each: 1. In the legacy approach, annotations themselves aren't stored in the archive, but their definitions are: look under `metadata.section_defs` in any `archive.json`. This means that to retrieve the annotations of a `Section` or `Quantity` definition, you need the module (`nomad-lab` or the plugin) that containing the definition. The reason for this, is that definitions used to be algorithmically enforced when reading / writing the archive. 2. In the new approach to schema building (using inheritance to extend base sections), part of the annotations are written to the archive at the level above the actual processed data. You can take a look at the `m_attributes` under `data` in [this example](https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/gui/search/eln/entry/id/P_JobvC719C4hlWaRZ9Db5Q-B_6U/data/data/Xps/ENTRY/0), either via the browser or the archive This currently only applies to experiments, though we are working on …

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
question: @ahmed ilyas Thank you for the explanation, and sorry for not acknowledging sooner. I think I am starting to understand this better, at least in principle. What I don't understand still is the following: In the toml that you linked, it says: `"nomad-parser-plugins-atomistic>=1.0"` But the requirements file says: `nomad-parser-plugins-atomistic==1.0.2 # via nomad-distribution (pyproject.toml)` It looks like these were updated a week ago. But the atomistic parsers has several versions above this, e.g., v1.0.3 from 3 weeks ago. Why is this not updating in the requirements and how can I fix this?
proposed_answer: The toml gives a valid set of distributions, the `requirements.txt` is generated using the `scripts/generate_python_dependencies.sh` script. But if you want a specific version of nomad-parser-plugins-atomistic in the requirements file, you can bump up the version in the toml file and generate the requirements txt file.

id: NOMAD - issues - Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop [1332313926045798452] (2025-01-01 to 2025-03-31)|q13-a14
thread_id: NOMAD - issues - Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop [1332313926045798452] (2025-01-01 to 2025-03-31)
title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
question: I don't want a specific version though, I just always want the latest release version. I thought this would already happen with `"nomad-parser-plugins-atomistic>=1.0"` in the toml. The question is why is this stuck at the 1.0.2 release?
proposed_answer: In the `scripts/generate_python_dependencies.sh` it will only upgrade nomad-lab and nomad-lab-gui, no plugins are upgraded automatically so they will always keep their locked version. The intention here was that no other dependency gets automatically updated and individual plugin authors can upgrade their plugins as they see fit and we have a more controlled roll out. Otherwise, if random plugins get updated automatically during the nightly run, the pipeline might start failing due to changes in the plugin.

question: Thanks for your answers, but I am still not really sure what I should do. I only have the 3 infrastructure containers (elastic, rabbitmq, mongo) running and don't see anything special in their logs. The rest is running in the terminal. @Lauri Himanen How can I check if the worker is running properly?
proposed_answer: You can check the status of the services by running `docker ps`. This will show "STATUS" column that you can check. Also, you could inspect the live logs by removing the -d flag from the docker compose command: `docker compose up` instead of `docker compose up -d`. This will show you all of the logs from all of the services. You could then copy/paste this whole log here and we can inspect them for any signs of possible failure.

question: I have another question about connecting NOMAD and NEXUS: when I upload a nexus file to nomad it creates a *nexus* subsection, and a *results* subsection for my entry. The *results* subsection has a *material* section which includes some things that I did include in my nexus file such as the "chemical formula hill" which I have in SAMPLE>SUBSTANCE>molecular_formula_hill . Is there a way to connect them somehow... or is it in the pipeline? Well, in general to connect directly this NXsubstance to the material section of nomad? Or does it have to be done exclusively through a separate entry. Also …
proposed_answer: The most frequent and current parsing of content from NeXus to NOMAD takes instance data from a few concepts in NeXus/HDF5 files and maps it to a nomad.metainfo.results section. The more recent pynxtools versions realize this via this code: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/pynxtools/blob/master/src/pynxtools/nomad/parser.py From arrays summary statistics are carried over. Newer versions of NOMAD in combination with pynxtools try to match each concept in the NeXus file to metainfo as you can explore via the "Data" tab of an Upload, however not all data irrespective of metadata or data are carried over to metainfo.results. That can be changed by changing either the above-mentioned parser or via using are forms of parsing Relating search: The search question is formulated not precise enough. Different energy regions for each spectrum, the same energy region for all of the spectra, etc.? Anyhow, such searching is currently not implemented. For several reasons, one breaking reason of which is that to realize such search it would need a functional evaluation whether the spectra at all contain the energy range unless these ranges have been defined a priori. As an example, say in your spect …

thread_id: NOMAD - issues - Fail to launch jupyter hub on oasis [1278335323348275303] (2024-07-01 to 2024-09-30)
title: Fail to launch jupyter hub on oasis
question: Unfortunately I am no longer on site with the users so will take a bit of time to check this but I'll ask the IT admin. For my understanding, is it that it tries to mount these folders in the jupyter container and then can't access them?
proposed_answer: But this comes from the nomad app not the jupyterhub. The stacktrace indicates that this error occures when the app tries to collect all upload directories before it sends them to jupyterhub. In other words the jupyterhub is not jet involved. It sounds more like a file permission problem as Lauri says. You sould docker exec into the app container and check: - does the upload directory exist in .volumes/fs/staging (and only there, not again in .volumes/fs/public) - does the upload directory belong to user 1000 - does the app actually run as user 1000 You can also run the `nomad admin uploads integrity --missing-storage` command in the container to check if there are uploads with missing (or inaccesible) directories.

title: Documentation
question: Hi Marcelo, that is correct. All entry points of the plugin should be listed under the corresponding type (schema packages, parsers, apps, etc) under "About this distribution". How did you install the plugins? Did you try to mount the plugin code directly (Option1 in the docs you linked) or did you install it with pip (Option 2)?
proposed_answer: Hi Hampus, I installed the [nomad-hysprint](https://github.com/nomad-hzb/nomad-hysprint/tree/main) plugin using [Option 1: Mount the plugin code](https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/docs/howto/oasis/plugins_install.html#option-1-mount-the-plugin-code). I was confused by documentation: it uses the path `nomad-schema-plugin-example/nomadschemaexample`, what I see is `plugin/nomad-hysprint/src/nomad_hysprint/parsers` and that is what I used in my `docker-compose.plugins.yaml:` ``` ./plugin/nomad-hysprint/src/nomad_hysprint/parsers:/app/plugins/src/nomad_hysprint/parsers ``` I noticed the `docker compose` took a longer then usual, but everything looked fine. I was expecting to find `hysprint` in the plugin section of NOMAD home page.

title: @Lukas Pielsticker and I just found a
question: If I understand, the correct solution is that it inherit sdirectly from the base class, and this is what is returned, doesn't it?
proposed_answer: Yes, the actual problem we have is an NXcalibration in NXprocess_mpes. So the inheritance chain should be [`NXmpes:/ENTRY/PROCESS_MPES/CALIBRATION`, `NXprocess_mpes:CALIBRATION`, `NXcalibration`] but I just got [`NXmpes:/ENTRY/PROCESS_MPES/CALIBRATION`, `NXcalibration`] . With calling `get_inherited_nodes` with path and root node this gives [`NXmpes:/ENTRY/PROCESS_MPES/CALIBRATION`, `NXprocess_mpes`, `NXcalibration`], which is fine but I want to store the nodes directly instead of their xml roots Anyways, I replaced this with my own implementation which works now With this we probably could also untie this from dev_tools code. We only use `get_nx_namefit` and `get_nexus_version` in pynx. `get_nexus_version` is just a fallback and not really necessary the way we use this with the `nexus-version.txt`

title: @Florian Dobener with new validation
question: Some link do not have any correct field or data path to be linked, Because the data is not provided by the raw file. Convertet can set a check, is the link to the data is available or not. If not found converter should remove the path that is linking to other data field. As this feature will be applied to all the readers, dataconverter can have this feature for all the readers. Does it make sense?
proposed_answer: This should be done by the reader When the dict ends up at validation this should already be resolved or deleted The first error: ``` WARNING - Field /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/current_amplifier/hardware written without documentation. WARNING - Field /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/hardware/@version written without documentation. ``` Is because `.../current_amplifier/hardware` is set as a field with a single value. In NXsts, however, it is a group of `NXfabrication` You probably need to add `/model` and `/model/@version` at the end

My research turned up nothing, this is apparently exactly how the mkdocs API is supposed to be accessed. Has anyone else encountered this issue and can point me towards a solution?
proposed_answer: Is mkdocs installed in your python environment? ``` pip show mkdocs ``` Maybe trying to reinstall nomad-lab could work. I vaguely remember having the same error at some point 🤔 Generally I just delete the environment, create a new one, and run the setup dev env script again when I run into these sort of issues 😅 I'm sorry, I just read your message again and saw that it's the setup script failing... 🤦‍♂️ Try deleting the environment (or using a new on) and see if that helps.

title: Scatterplot: focus on region
question: Can I tests this in the Oasis? Which scatter plot? Note that when you select a region in the scatter plot, you are essentially setting a filter like `500 K < temperature < 600 K AND 50 < conversion < 80`. If `temperature` is an array, this will include entries where temperature is e.g. [300, 400, 500]. But maybe first let me know how to reproduce this and then we can see if there is indeed a bug, or if the behaviour is simply unexpected and should be reconsidered.
proposed_answer: Yes, you can test this in the[Test Oasis](https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/oasis/gui/search/heterogeneouscatalyst?results.properties.catalytic.reaction.reaction_conditions.temperature%5Bgte%5D=510.1864404632021%20K&results.properties.catalytic.reaction.reaction_conditions.temperature%5Blte%5D=640.2867625380519%20K) it does show the first filter, e.g. for the temperature, but not the other axis, e.g. the feed composition, it also does not update the range that it shows in the widget. partly it is because an entry that falls within the search window also has data points outside. but there is some problem for the 2nd axis, probably because it is not specified further , e.g.reactants[*].conversion so it looks like it does not attempt any filtering along the 2nd axis

question: @Lauri Himanen do you know what might be happening here?

I think you are following the `nomad-distro-dev` setup in which Elasticsearch is run through docker, and then the app+worker are run as a separate python process that is outside docker. Is this right? I'm not famiiliar with WSL and cannot really test this myself as I don't have a Windows machine available. Are you using Docker desktop wih the WSL2 backend? You could try the following: - If `docker compose` and `uv run poe start` are run in different WSL distributions, they cannot communicate by default. You could check this by running the command `wsl -l -v` in both terminals to verify that they run in the same WSL distribution. Also you could check if you can run `docker ps` in the terminal which you use to run `uv run poe start`.

title: Proxies resolve_variadic_name to get_nx_...
question: These problems with namefitting are with the get_nx_namefit addition?
proposed_answer: I pushed the changes See my message in the area-b devs chat Still, even if make the metainfo generation work, enabling the tests in test_parsing.py fails anyway.. By the way, even when we use `get_nx_namefit`, we still have the problem that the field `delays` in NXdata gets matched to `DATA` and not to `AXISNAME`. It is really because of the inconsistency in NXdata that there are two uppercase concept that can generally only be resolved on the parsing level (through `signal` and `axes`), but not on the schema level. This is a general flaw of NXdata 🤨

id: NOMAD - Synthesis & Experiment - general-expt [1201461908117663815] (2024-10-01 to 2024-12-31)|q3-a4
thread_id: NOMAD - Synthesis & Experiment - general-expt [1201461908117663815] (2024-10-01 to 2024-12-31)
title: general-expt
question: Q: is it possible at the moment, from nexus entries to somehow link to other nomad entries, such as materials which are already documented in NOMAD?
proposed_answer: You could use the workflow in order to connect different entries with different upload_id and entry_id. You would do that by creating a new entry and defining the workflow in there (this is a `workflow.archive.yaml` file). This is totally agnostic of nexus, but you can add the path to the nxs mainfile in your workflow and hence keep the provenance of Nexus entry + material in Nomad.

id: NOMAD - Synthesis & Experiment - general-expt [1201461908117663815] (2024-10-01 to 2024-12-31)|q5-a6
thread_id: NOMAD - Synthesis & Experiment - general-expt [1201461908117663815] (2024-10-01 to 2024-12-31)
title: general-expt
question: Thank you. just realized I already had the same question a few months ago :p although formulated differently. Got a different solution back then as I was proposed to just add the id of my older entry in the nexus file of my new entry. I assume the workflow offers me the additional possibility to connect entries after they are both already entered, right?
proposed_answer: Yes, you can even define a `workflow2` section inside the nxs entry. Workflow per se is just a way of organizing data, so you can decide how to do it at will. You can either decide to use the nxs entries as your basic building block and work with workflows **on top of it**, creating more entries using the `workflow.archive.yaml` I told you OR, you can put everything in the nxs entry by rewritting the nxs file with `entry_id`s. The decision here is just pure data model design, so you are free to go with whichever you prefer. Both options (breaking into multiple entries for workflows vs. all in one entry) have pros and cons. In Area A (synthesis) and C (computation), we go with the first option in a slightly different way.

title: Announcement:
question: @ahmed ilyas Trying to install, I get that the `alabaster==1.0.0` dependency requires Python `>=3.10` Given backwards-compatibility with `3.9`, should this dependency then be removed?
proposed_answer: How are you trying to install? If it's with the `requirements.txt` file, note that this is generated for `py3.11`. What I mean by supporting `py3.9` is that, if you have a `py3.9` installation you should be able to pip install the latest nomad. This is useful for specially for external users who may just want to `pip install nomad-lab` to query some data.

question: In my local everything works fine, in the IKZ Oasis I get this error: in load_archive raise MetainfoReferenceError( nomad.metainfo.metainfo.MetainfoReferenceError: Referencing another Upload is not allowed. Can I ask you more info on why this happens?
proposed_answer: It is quite hard to say without additional information. Looks like the schema/parser is doing some actions in the `ServerContext` that causes this, not sure if it is a bug or a feature. Maybe @Theodore Chang DM BITTE has more insight but I think we would need some way to reproduce this.

title: discussion
question: Hi, I have a Section with a Quantity `process` of type `Reference(BaseProcess.m_def)` . In a `normalize `function I would now like to access the `entry_id` of the referenced file. Is there a method which can do this, like` self.process.m_... (’entry_id’)`?
proposed_answer: Hi Aaron: The combination of defining a `Package` and calling its `__init__metainfo__()` -method simply registers the definitions in that file into our system. If you leave these parts out, I think you can still regularly use the schema in your Python code, but the definitions will not be visible in our GUI code (=you can't e.g. instantiate entries from that schema). We hope that this package registration can be automated in a revision of the plugin system that we are working on.

question: To write a task in the `worfklow.yaml` file, we always need an input file and an ouptut file, as such: ```yaml tasks: - m_def: nomad.datamodel.metainfo.workflow.TaskReference task: '../upload/archive/mainfile/NOMAD_upload_23/MOL9/MOL18/MOL9_240_MOL18_160/minimEQ1.log#/workflow2' name: GeometryOpt inputs: - name: input system section: '../upload/archive/mainfile/NOMAD_upload_23/MOL9/MOL18/MOL9_240_MOL18_160/minimEQ1.log#/run/0/system/0' outputs: - name: relaxed system section: '../upload/archive/mainfile/NOMAD_upload_23/MOL9/MOL18/MOL9_240_MOL18_160/minimEQ1.log#/run/0/system/-1' - name: energy …
proposed_answer: The schema is quite general. I think you can have a task without an input. @ChemaP ? btw - these references are to sections of the archive, not technically to files. id: NOMAD - issues - NotImplementedError for context.raw_path_exists method [1214492993151836240] (2024-01-01 to 2024-03-31)|q0-a1
thread_id: NOMAD - issues - NotImplementedError for context.raw_path_exists method [1214492993151836240] (2024-01-01 to 2024-03-31)
title: NotImplementedError for context.raw_path_exists method
question: When running nomad installation with app_worker, the `m_context` is an instance of the class `nomad.datamodel.context.ServerContext` which has a well-defined `raw_path_exists` method. But while running pytests, which uses the nomad as a package (and not the local installation), `m_context` is an instance of class `nomad.datamodel.context.Context` in which `raw_path_exists` is not implemented. Because of this difference, the pytests fail during the plugin testing, when using `archive.m_context.raw_path_exists(file_path)`. Can we harmonize these two methods for the classes `ServerContext` and `C …
proposed_answer: I guess we only implemented the Context interface based on what was necessary. The ServerContext is the more complete one as this is used all the time in the app. The ClientContext is only used by, well, clients. E.g. when you do the "nomad parse" command, or the ArchiveQuery instantiates metainfo object froms the retrieved data. For the first case "nomad parse" you commit seems ok. For the second one, it does not hurt, but it also does not help, as the raw data is most likely not locally available. I guess it complements the existing `raw...` functions. Feel free to create an MR and merge this.

id: NOMAD - forum-comp - Support for quantum technology_quantum information (QuTiP) [1337020383588647003] (2025-01-01 to 2025-03-31)|q0-a1
thread_id: NOMAD - forum-comp - Support for quantum technology_quantum information (QuTiP) [1337020383588647003] (2025-01-01 to 2025-03-31)
title: Support for quantum technology/quantum information (QuTiP)
question: Original message from @MMCasto : https://discordapp.com/channels/1201445470485106719/1201461948433313792/1330908831298883594 @Esma Boydas Could you look briefly in QuTiP (https://qutip.readthedocs.io/en/qutip-5.1.x/) to see what the scope is and if there is any overlap with Quanty (naively it seems to me there is some) @MMCasto What I can say is that there is atm no existing schema for Quantum Technology, but there are some components that may be developed within our new schema and with other collaborations, e.g., for wavefunction storage (we are currently having a similar discussion with the …
proposed_answer: There is indeed overlap in terms of the basic QM concepts & operations, but they have different flavors & focuses and end-applications. QuTiP is a more general framework, it has more applications related to quantum information and similar subfields, e.g. see scQubits (https://scqubits.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) or Pulser (https://pulser.readthedocs.io/en/latest/). Quanty is more specialized for many-body physics and spectroscopy, e.g. my experience with Quanty was when I was modelling L3 XAS and RIXS of first-row transition metal containing complexes. It seems like we could start off by touching up the NOMAD schema so that we can fully encompass wave-function representations, quantum states and so on. Before we move ahead with unifying these two efforts, since QuTiP is more general and @MMCasto has already mentioned putting down a schema by SISSA, it would be great if we can learn more about the extent of the schema they have in mind

question: Dear Joe, thanks for the link. If I follow your link I do not see the data directly, but a page with more links. One of them is a link to "energies = 301 vector". If I click this I get a list of 301 energies. I must be missing something as I have a problem to see a vector with the values. (?). What I would need to implement a method for Quanty to read DFT output from Nomad, (so that we can interface one data format instead of a different format for each code) is the local partial density of states, i.e. DOS seperated to sites and atomic orbitals. Am I correct that the amount of data stored dep …
proposed_answer: When you click on the 301 energies you should be able to see the data vector...but this is just a browser tool for casually exploring the data populated in a NOMAD archive (archive == serialized version of the data within an entry, according to the NOMAD schema). You can download the entire archive "manually" via the download button toward the top right of the page. Then you will have a json file with all of the data within that entry. You can also achieve this with an API call. You can query the NOMAD database for certain types of entries and then grab either their entire archives, or specific quantities, e.g., energies. I can try to direct you towards some resources for this, but it would help me to understand better what you would like to tackle and in what order. We've now discussed several different tasks/goals: extending NOMAD to support some Quanty structures, a parser plugin for Quanty calculations/workflows, and utilizing the data already on NOMAD for new calculations / analysis.

proposed_answer: there is not much aside from the docstrings ``` mainfile_contents_dict: A nested dictionary to match the contents of the file. title: discussion
question: Hello ! I have a question concerning the persistence of the data in NOMAD central. It is said that data are kept for at least 10 years and metadata always accessible. So let's jump in 10 years, for a dataset published today, what will remain of the entry ? I assume the `results`, `data`, `run`, `workflow*`, `nexus` sections will be removed and the `metadata` section will remain here for ever, is that correct ?
proposed_answer: The problem is on how you are writing the data section. You should not have the curly brackets `{ }` for the string type quantities. It should look like this: ```json { "name": "entity test", "datetime": "2024-10-09T07:11:26.825938+00:00", "lab_id": "HUB_PeMa_20241009_instrument-test" } ```

title: discussion
question: **Custom schemas for defining sub-sections in entry ELNs** It seems that the schemas loaded by uploading schema YAML archives are only available under the custom schemas to create entries. However, they are not available in the dropdown menu for initializing sub-sections in an entry. Is this by design? Can we extend the custom schemas to create sub-sections?
proposed_answer: About HDF5 datasets in NOMAD. HDF5Dataset is a class available in NOMAD, it stores huge arrays in an hdf5 file connected to a NOMAD entry. This means handling huge data without slowing down the processing, as usually experienced when storing directly in json or yaml. It is also possible to retrieve the arrays in an easy and compact way and have them available in, e.g., a normalize function, as shown here https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/staging/docs/howto/customization/hdf5.html#hdf5dataset Possible uses of these deserialized arrays is further processing or plotting with the integrated plotly functionalities. One question: does plotting huge arrays with plotly nullifies the efforts of putting them in a hdf5 file? Should I avoid it and just lean on the integrated h5web tool for array visualization? Any hint @mohammad.nakhaee @Lauri Himanen ? The image attached shows a plotly plot in a subsection containing two hdf5 arrays. The code for this solution is available here https://github.com/PDI-Berlin/pdi-nomad-plugin/blob/31633711c7fc9b7cf98024358a9d0f13eb34b208/src/pdi_nomad_plugin/mbe/processes.py#L503.

question: Hi guys! Question: are the "Sample" and "Users" fields standardized across experiments? Because typically you would do several experiments on the same batch right? How do you communicate that across experiments?
proposed_answer: Depends on the data model used: i) for basesections https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/staging/docs/howto/customization/base_sections.html concepts are offered that can be used to refer to what is typically considered a "sample", so yeah "sample" is standardized. Basesections offer in-build functionalities @Hampus Näsström and @Andrea Albino can give you more details here. ii) For the NeXus data model concepts "sample" and "user" - among many other concepts - (instrument, data) https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nexus_definitions/tree/fairmat/base_classes are standardized, with the design choice that in FAIRmat we agreed that all NeXus-based data models build domain and technique-specific data models using the existent NeXus base classes. When makiung changes to these is required or desired, there is a standardized process for discussing such suggestions and come to an agreement with the global NeXus community. NOMAD internally uses the metainfo data model. Basesections write content to metainfo, instance data using the NeXus data model is mapped when NOMAD parses these instance data provided they come from experimental techniques known to NOMAD (e.g. EM, MPES, XPS, XRD, OPTICAL, APT, SPS) …

id: NOMAD - issues - Problems with data types after updating to NOMAD develop branch [1259797835067691109] (2024-07-01 to 2024-09-30)|q2-a3
thread_id: NOMAD - issues - Problems with data types after updating to NOMAD develop branch [1259797835067691109] (2024-07-01 to 2024-09-30)
title: Problems with data types after updating to NOMAD develop branch
question: This is due to the new metainfo datatypes merged recently. And yes they are less forgiving now. Auto converting strings into numbers is really a stretch. This conversion from string to float is really the parsers job. @Theodore Chang DM BITTE whats your opinion on this?
proposed_answer: The type system internally tries to convert the input data into the target type. In this particular case, when a string "20" is given, the type instance tries to check the following: "20" == str(numpy.float64("20")), this results actual check to be something like "20" == "20.000000000000012" due to the inexact nature of floats. The new system tries to be as smart as possible but for cases like this, the risk of loss of precision cannot be avoided. I'll say the easy fix is assign the correct value to the quantity, meaning that instead of "20", use numpy.float64("20").

thread_id: NOMAD - forum-comp - Upload Multiple Datasets (Each with a workflow) [1392073923784343634] (2025-07-01 to 2025-09-30)
title: Upload Multiple Datasets (Each with a workflow)
question: @Julia Schumann Do you mean that I should upload everything and later add `workflow.archive.yaml` manually in all datasets? How did you do it with NEB data?
proposed_answer: 1) You should indeed put several datasets together at once. The limit for each upload is 32Gb 2) I think Aakash had a similar issue, and there is no solution except for uploading the workflow.archive.yaml after the other files, like Julia suggested. I think Joe is aware of this, so there will hopefully be some changes in the parsing infrastructure. Now, regarding the folders structure, I would suggest to directly put everything in the same folder, or at least, the Wannier being in a subfolder. If you do that, you could even get rid off the need of the workflow.archive.yaml (cc @Alvin Noe Ladines) and try to recognize the workflow automatically. The reason for this is that, having a serial step workflow means that the steps can be kept in the same folder without creating any burden. Parallel steps need to be separated in parallel folders. This way you maximize what you can do programatically with Python and parsers 🙂

question: @Leo Beck as you have discovered, `atoms_info` is being populated by the MDAnalysis parser. If `atoms_info.name` is `none`, that means that this information is not being parsed by MDAnalysis (i.e., not inside of the MDAnalysis universe). If you check the universe created for one of the nomad tests (e.g., hexane_cyclohexane), you will see that there is atom name information in there. Then, if you want to understand how the names (labels/elements) were found by MDAnalysis in that case, you should check ```python self._file_handler = MDAnalysis.Universe( self.mainfile, *self.auxilliary_files, **s …

question: I think @Micha knows some tricks about this. BTW, are you working with spin bot by any chance? Any thin film materials?
proposed_answer: Hey @yrrliu i usually create new files through parsing and use the normalizer to read the data and move data around. you can create entries in a normalizer the same ways as in a parser., but usually for me at parsing i already know how many files should be created for uploaded data. what we do for example is we have an excel sheet where each row is the synthesis for one sample with multiple synthesis processes, we then: 1. upload excel sheet 2. check with is_mainfile if it has the right structure 3. iterate over each row and create a sample entry and multiple synthesis processes with the data in the excel sheet 4. link the synthesis processes to the sample (keep in mind if you reference in one direction nomad lets the referenced entry know that it has been linked for queryiing later) later we then upload measurement data to the sample but this is then file by file based. i also suspect the issues with the reference being becaus of the client context 🙂

proposed_answer: we figured out that the trick is: ```python tank = "manual tank" query = { "owner": "visible", "query": { "entry_type": "PVcomB_HFDip", "upload_id": "3HK8UmRCR3OhVqQySD1kAA", "search_quantities": { "id": "data.process.used_tank#nomad_pvcomb.schema_packages.pvcomb_schema_package.PVcomB_HFDip", "str_value:all":tank.split(" ") } }} ``` which is really nice, it is good to know that single words are indexed 🙂

question: Good questions! I don't think there is a definitive right and wrong way to do it. You can edit the `coauthor_groups` of the upload over the API as you are pushing the data but I'm not sure if there is a way to disable the publishing option. Are the users meant to modify the sample entries later or is it just a reference? In case they are just referencing them you could just set the user groups as reviewers instead. If you don't know beforehand who will receive each sample I would probably put them in one big upload together with the sputter process. But maybe @Micha has some experience of doin …
proposed_answer: hey, the question from my side would be if the 50 samples are used in the same project and all further activity belonging together? I have learned that ideally the full story of samples belonging together should be in one upload with all measurements and processes together. If you produce samples not knowing where they will belong to, iw ould recommend having one upload for the samples with the standard processes and one upload for each subgroup of these samples containing all activities. Ideally there is also something like an `ExperimentalPlan` nomad schema which links samples from different uploads to a specific experimental series. with this the automatic uploader can do the following: 1. query for sample by name 2. get experimetnal plan the sample is in 3. upload the data to the upload with the experimental plan

question: Hi @Sarthak Kapoor @Alvin Noe Ladines I will try this out! Thanks a lot! 😃 @Alvin Noe Ladines @Sarthak Kapoor adding hdf5_file = Quantity( type=HDF5Reference, ) to schema and data.hdf5_file = f'CAMELS_data/{sample_name}/{self._fname}#/{self.camels_entry_name}/data' in the parser produces the HDF5 view in the DATA page as seen in figure hdf5_file.png and if I add the following in schema hdf5_view = Quantity( type=HDF5Reference, description='HDF5 view', a_h5web=H5WebAnnotation( paths=['/'], title='HDF5 File Visualization', ), ) and assign a path to root section in the parser data.hdf5_view = f'C …
proposed_answer: HI, you should add only a quantity that will contain the reference to your data and in the root section def add the annotation, ```class MySection(EntryData): m_def = Section(a_h5web=H5WebAnnotation(axes='time', signal='value')) value = Quantity( type=HDF5Dataset, unit='dimensionless', shape=[], a_h5web=H5WebAnnotation( long_name='power', errors='value_e' ), ) archive.data = MySection(value='{filename}#{path_to_data}') ```

question: Hi, is there a way to make a search term widget in an app to show more then 5 hits when using the text input box? Sometimes we have more results and its hard to find them by just typing... Changed the channel name: search widget showing more then 5 hits? Related to the original question above: I was testing the use of filters. There I can define the amount of entries that gets listed by `options=10`. Unfortunately, the custom filter menu behaves differntly to the search widget and does not show suggestions when start typing in a string. I could not find any hint in the docs if this is supporte …
proposed_answer: Suggestions are not available for custom quantities, but are collected for the shared quantities like entry_name, if so specified in their setup. So: Lab ID is a custom quantity => no suggestions `entry_name` is one of the quantities shared across schemas, and it has been configured to collect suggestions => suggestions are shown

thread_id: NOMAD - issues - DEV Deployment stalls on upload [1331212145701949491] (2025-01-01 to 2025-03-31)
title: DEV Deployment stalls on upload
question: Typically when an upload hangs it is because of resources: the processing e.g. runs out of memory or crashes in some other unexpected reason. If possible, this should then be debugged locally. Also the `develop`-deployment is not really equitpped with very beefy machines, so trying to process anything very large there can be problematic. @Joseph Rudzinski: Are these all uploads that are left in a limbo state because of failed processing? They are not published or anything?
proposed_answer: yeah, they are all just failed processing, unpublished tests of mine. I think in the early days we did have the case that you describe above, where there were really some bugs in the e.g., parsers or GUI. But I haven't experienced anything like that in a long time. In all the cases I experience nowadays (and several others have had the same experience), something goes wrong on the infrastructure that causes the processing to hang. Reuploading the file a 2nd time results in successful processing. I do not think there is anything extraordinary about these uploads. The size may be larger than a typical DFT calculation, but in most cases uploads of this size are processing fine. It could be that there is some sort of memory bug or something that is subtle, but in my experience the hanging is somewhat rare and random, so I wouldn't really want to spend time digging into this. Perhaps we can start keeping a more detailed log of these issues to try to find any correlations.

title: NXiv_temp error
question: I remember discussing with Theodore where he wanted to allow all users to give a custom resolver. I'm wrapped up with some tasks right now. I can have a look in the evening. But if you're already checking, we're not using our resolver for this?
proposed_answer: I do not think we do, but I have not checked it deeper. If you could check in the evening that would be great. Checking it now deeper, I see that m_get is called which is calling _ensure_definition which is then calling resolve_variadic_name in line 1197. Note that resolve variadic_name only receives a hint (and does only check name fit, and not even proper type match), so it seems that custom resolver is not yet implemented. It seems that NXiv_temp is a good example to test these inheritance issues. Thanks for checking it in the evening!

title: Hello everyone , I would like to know if
question: Our goal is to build an application that handles settings from various instruments, each with unique configuration requirements. Each type of instrument needs a custom set of settings, ideally organized dynamically based on the instrument type. Given this diversity in configurations, would there be a way to make the plugin more flexible within the existing static schema approach, or would you suggest another method to manage these varied settings effectively?
proposed_answer: do you talk about some instrument like a table-top hand crafted one, such as the one built for non linear spectroscopy experiments and other things that is hard to structure because non standard? You may want to check how deep in structuring you want to go and which is the level of structuring needed for you to consume that data afterwards. This is very important. Maybe a flat class where you list your sensors timeseries or other arrays in enough for you. Base classes approach is useful on the other hand, e.g. "divide et impera" approach. What I mean is that maybe your setup is not standard but the single pieces you mount in can be, i.e. a monochromator, a beam splitter, etc. In that case you can record these metadata in the baseclass and then list it in a flat fashion into a generic instrument class. Do you get my point? Maybe talking in abstract does not help. Can you provide a description of what you want to do?

question: Concerning using `#` as a custom separator in a url: According to [RFC3986](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3986#appendix-A), the "#" sign is reserved as a separator for the fragment. But according to [a stackoverflow post](https://stackoverflow.com/a/10851032), browsers automatically convert subsequent `#` to `%23`. Is this the problem?
proposed_answer: We should then follow the RFC for sure. I can try always encoding the hash sign as %23 when setting it through the GUI in the URL: this way it should hopefully not interfere with keycloak.

What I was suggesting was more like "I need this asap, so I want to merge it in the schema_package of my parser to test it, refine it, and when it is ready move it to nomad-simulations". In any other case, it should be directly in nomad-simulations. The origin of this discussion was a label "urgent" in the nomad-simulations repo

question: Thanks, I understand better now. And just in case, a normalizer entry point is used to modulate an archive placed as input, right?
proposed_answer: Yes, Normalizer entry points contain Python code that act on archives, not on files like parsers do. Normalizer entry points can be used to e.g. automate tasks that are common for the entire Oasis or for a specific set of schemas.

question: FMI: Are runners warning when their certificate is about to expire?
proposed_answer: The k8s cluster sub-systems use certificates for almost all communitcation. This than also extends to things that want to use the cluster, e.g. the gitlab runner needs the certs to start pods for all the pipeline steps. The k8s certs are only valid for one year. The k8s gods just designed it this way. Basically, our cluster celebrated its 1st birthday today at 10:12h. We still have to figure out how to automatize this in the future (or at least do this intime).

question: I have a local test oasis running on my laptop and when I create a new upload and then reload the page it only says “Could not validate credentials. The given token is invalid. (401)“ and the upload page is not shown anymore. This did not happen on my linux computer before, but I moved to a windows computer now and here the problem emerged. I changed nothing in the docker compose file. Any spontaneous ideas what could be the problem here? Maybe someone of you has experienced this before?
proposed_answer: No, no spontaneous ideas. We would need more insight, but I am afraid this will be really hard to troubleshoot remotely. This happens directly after clicking create upload? Or only after refreshing the browser window? What happens when you open the oasis in another tab?

title: Cannot access uploads, because of error: Could not validate credentials. The given token is invalid.
question: No, no spontaneous ideas. We would need more insight, but I am afraid this will be really hard to troubleshoot remotely. This happens directly after clicking create upload? Or only after refreshing the browser window? What happens when you open the oasis in another tab?
proposed_answer: Does it persist? I get some weird timeout errors when working from home with the NOMAD keycloak which gives the same error. Usually waiting a minute and then closing the error resolves this. If that doesn't work, could you check the logs and see if there is some more info there?

id: NOMAD - issues - Cannot access uploads, because of error: Could not validate credentials. The given token is invalid. [1224721042388553808] (2024-04-01 to 2024-06-30)|q4-a5
thread_id: NOMAD - issues - Cannot access uploads, because of error: Could not validate credentials. The given token is invalid. [1224721042388553808] (2024-04-01 to 2024-06-30)
title: Cannot access uploads, because of error: Could not validate credentials. The given token is invalid.
question: `The token is not yet valid (iat)` sounds like a time(zone) issue. E.g. our user management and your oasis not running in sync. Your laptop being behind. When you open a new tab, you start a new instance of the gui and it will get a new (potentially out of time) token. So every time you open a new tab there is the potential for this to happen. Could you check your os time settings? I am not sure what the Gateway timeout problem is. Is the gateway timeout coming from keycloak or the nomad api?
proposed_answer: Perfect, I think the time issue was indeed the problem. I went to the OS settings and synchronized the clock again. From then on I did not yet get the validation error message again. This also explains why it sometimes worked and sometimes not, because from time to time the OS is synchronizing the clock automatically. Thank you very much. The Gateway Timeout Error is coming from the NOMAD API. But in the container logs there is no information about it. Maybe it was due to the Validation error, e.g. if I had no valid token, I was not allowed to access some entries and run into a timeout. I will keep an eye on that.

title: ab-initio
question: Let's then separate the pure atom state of an element in a cell, from the algebraic basis used to solve the model. If agreed, where should PP really go?
proposed_answer: Conceptually, PP is a form of electron representation for the core-electrons (similar to a basis set, which may include both or just valence electrons). Structure-wise, it will follow the same data pattern as atom parameters. Maybe, we want a hierarchy system for electronic representation that lives side-by-side of the geometric one?

question: When the nexus parser wants to parse calibration_time (/entry/instrument/environment/current_sensor/calibration_time), it actually finds that this data field should belong to FABRICATION. The problem is with inheritance: current_sensor has not been recognised as specialisation of NXsensor_scan/ENTRY/INSTRUMENT/ENVIRONMENT/SENSOR but only of NXsensor Note that this inheritance is properly picked up in nexus_definitions Note that I have already found this error in Sept: https://discord.com/channels/1201445470485106719/1209870497211351090/1285326069737590794
proposed_answer: I thought we now use the same code in Nomad and Nexus Defs to figure out inheritance. I can take a look if we didn't actually resolve this. There was a branch up on gitlab. It's now gone. I will later check the local changes I have. Maybe that was merged in by someone else.

question: In May, we discussed battery workflows tailored to my case (SCF + NEB). I believe @ChemaP suggested the initial draft. Later, in a meeting with @Julia Schumann , we decided to simplify the workflow by removing the SCF calculation, focusing solely on the NEB workflow. For my specific case, Chema proposed a comprehensive battery workflow that includes both SCF and NEB calculations. The final version of the NEB workflow was established, and I’m currently using both workflows separately. At this point, adding a plot should complete the work on my end. In your opinion, what additional properties co …
proposed_answer: Hi @Uday Gajera I don't think I can answer your question yet, since I am still trying to understand your project scope and goals. Just for some background, @Pepe Marquez and I were talking and think that Area E can benefit from some collaboration since there are overlapping tasks. This is clear in what you have discussed with Julia and Chema, but I am trying to possibly help organize this together with the Area C efforts. A few questions then to help get me caught up: 1. You mentioned in after your cafe talk that ~1/2 of your data is on NOMAD. Do you have an Oasis? I don't see anything in the central repo. title: llm-projects
question: So, we have only a handful (61) different DOI's referenced to in the NOMAD Archive. I suppose this is not enough to train the word-embedding model. However, the fact that these DOI's are mentioned in NOMAD is not crucial. What about gathering a larger set of titles+abstracts on the basis of keywords? Sina provided a list of those abstracts and my plan is to identify a few keywords to filter the abstracts in CrossRef and use the result for the training. @Kevin Jablonka Does this look reasonable? If so, I can go through the abstracts and start with a list of keywords and then we see how many abs …
proposed_answer: We have these "user-provided" metadata in NOMAD: comments and references. Those are not well integrated into metainfo and archive, they are often not well utilised, and have bad cost/benefit in general. Combined with other plans like combining the upload and dataset concepts, they should be overhauled eventually. During this process, it would make sense to formalize "references" better. E.g. introduce types of references and checks like the proposed "does it resolve."

title: Metainfo Browser for Schema Plugins
question: The normal metainfo browser should show plugins as a "source" when installed. But I guess you want something, where you don't have a nomad installation and just get a browser, e.g. `nomad admin run metainfo-browser`?
proposed_answer: yeah, something like that. The main case we had in mind is that a user wants to understand the schema structure that is included within a plugin, e.g., from the standard simulation schema used in the central NOMAD (this is linked to how we should add the new schemas that we are developing to the corresponding plugin docs, which is why the question was initially raised). But this then generalizes to a tool where anyone can visualize the schema that they are building, which is nice. In the past, we were always talking about how to point users to the sections/quantities of the metainfo that are actually relevant to their domain. With the move to schema plugins this is now straightforward with such a tool

question: The problem here is that you are mixing `plugins.include/plugins.options` + `plugins.entry_pionts.include/options`. You can use either, but not both at the same time. The reason is that our config never merges lists, only overwrites. In this case `options.include` overwrites `plugins.entry_points.include`. Could you try if you can just move `pvbcombschema` to `plugins.entry_points`?
proposed_answer: you mean 'schemas/pvcombschema' or just pvcombschema

title: Mapping Annotation Parser Questions and Discussion
question: So, it doesn't seem to be an issue with defining my own ModelSystem class and, thus, also I guess the proxy is not an issue -- I removed the custom class, and use `model_system.ModelSystem....` for all the annotations, so my sub_systems annotation looks like: ```python from nomad_simulations.schema_packages import model_system model_system.ModelSystem.sub_systems.m_annotations.setdefault('mapping', {})['hdf5'] = ( MapperAnnotation( mapper=('get_sub_systems', ['.@'], dict(path='connectivity.particles_group')) ) ) ``` but `get_sub_systems` is still not called 🤔 Any other ideas for debugging?
proposed_answer: you can import this class and see if the annotations are really added in the original ModelSystem. I have looked at your plugin and it seems you are using your custom simulation class. this inherits the model_system sub section which uses the original ModelSystem class. But sub_systems sub-section is given by SectionProxy('ModelSystem') which I beleve targets the ModelSystem in the new class therefore causing the inconsistency. so redefining also the model_system sub-section under the new Simulation class explicitly using the new ModelSystem class will resolve the issue

title: Mapping Annotation Parser Questions and Discussion
question: So it is the parser's responsibility to set the max depth? What is the (negative?) impact of setting it higher by default, given now that the auto-nesting is deactivated?
proposed_answer: Setting it to a higher value will add a bit of an overhead as it will build a mapper up the to the depth specified. The mapping itself will simply return if no quantities are assigned so no impact. I also do not like this, but I think we should delegate this task to the parser as it has more info as to how many levels are required.

The current solution is to design the quantities for the min and max value and then searching them in the research app. In this way if an user will search for an equipment that works with an x value of pressure, it can be searched by imposing the mimum values of the entries less or equal to the value desired and the maximum value greater or equal to x. title: oasis
question: hey is it possible as an admin to mark an upload as unpublishable so that it can not be published by accident?
proposed_answer: There is no such functionality present at the moment. Are you encountering this a lot? We do have several warnings about what publishing means when you press the button: > Confirm that you want to publish the upload > You are about the publish this upload. The upload cannot be removed and the files and entries in this upload cannot be changed after publication. Maybe we need to make it even more clear to the user that this is more of an "archiving" operation. This is the direction we are going with the new GUI as well.

title: `nomad-docs`
question: Hi @everyone, We’ve moved the NOMAD documentation to a new repository on GitHub: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-docs. As part of the upcoming v1.3.16 release, we plan to remove the documentation from the `nomad-lab` package. The package size is already approaching 200 MB, and we want to avoid hitting PyPI’s size limits. From now on, please contribute to the documentation by creating pull requests (PRs) directly on the new GitHub repository. This will also make it easier for contributors outside the MPCDF GitLab to get involved. If you currently have an open merge request (MR) for docume …

title: discussion
question: @Julia Schumann **RUFF UPDATE**: The ruff version and specifications have been updated, which applies automatically for the plugin pipelines. Make sure to update your ruff locally, version 0.11.4 works for me. You may need to clear your ruff cache or do a full reinstall of your environment in the extreme case. The new import errors for `I001` that you might have seen can be fixed automatically with `ruff check . --fix`. @ahmed ilyas please check / anything to add?
proposed_answer: If youre using distro dev, uv sync —upgrade-package ruff will upgrade it to the latest version

question: It is not ideal because you can only write strings into the values of the XML tags. See discussion here as well: https://discordapp.com/channels/1201445470485106719/1209870497211351090/1341445362321199229. The idea is that you say ```xml <attribute name="vector" type="NX_NUMBER"> <enumeration> <item value="[0, 0, 1]"/> </enumeration> </attribute> ``` and then that means that a) you need to supply a list where each individual value in the list must be of type NX_NUMBER and b) that list much match [0, 0, 1], i.e. a list of numbers This is also how @Rubel Mozumder has it now implemented in pynxto …
proposed_answer: I understand the issue. I was just trying to ask what the expected behavior is for this. So "[]" -> list("[]") -> for any in list("[]")?

question: @Akhil I will add one more comment: an upload that is stuck in processing is not necessarily an indication that there is a problem, e.g., that your files are too large. In my experience this stalled processing is quite random and not so common. I agree that larger uploads are probably more prone to the issue, but typically one can just reupload again without issues. Indeed as @Pepe Marquez said, our infrastructure admins need to delete the stuck upload. We are working on such transient processing errors, but please do let us know if you continue to experience similar issues. For my records: co …

question: Haven't looked into that issue yet, will do a bit later I am a bit confused here. The AXISNAME field is present in NXdata, which ENTRY/data inherits from while energy field is defined directly in ENTRY/data Are they somehow the same or one overwritting the other?

id: NOMAD - discussion - I've just made a surprising discovery on [1327309371386105908] (2025-01-01 to 2025-03-31)|q2-a3
thread_id: NOMAD - discussion - I've just made a surprising discovery on [1327309371386105908] (2025-01-01 to 2025-03-31)
title: I've just made a surprising discovery on
question: there's something tricky with the tags here. `setuptools_scm` uses the tag info to determine which version to use. ```console ❯ git describe --tags v0.2.1-1-g0c0caad ``` Which matches the version output in the `uv pip check`. Not sure how this happened but when I look at the timestamps of the recent tags, it looks like `v0.3.1` and `v0.2.1` were created at the same time instant. ``` ❯ git for-each-ref --sort=creatordate --format '%(refname:short) %(creatordate)' refs/tags v0.0.0 Tue Jun 25 11:28:39 2024 +0200 v0.0.1 Tue Jun 25 15:45:07 2024 +0200 v0.0.2 Tue Jul 2 15:08:38 2024 +0200 v0.0.3 Mon …
proposed_answer: That would explain it, thank you! No, it doesn't cause any issue. I found this by chance while trying to fix a different issue that turned out to be caused by something unrelated, and thought it was intriguing.
