On a recent TF meeting we discussed it, and also that this limitation should be released at least for the required (or recommended) quantities. Yes. Although, in most cases a support for simple subclasses would do the harmonisation. So it would be enough to search for data.Entry.start_time since ALL nexus data are already preharmonised for this. Entry id is a deterministic id based on upload id and file name so as long as upload id is the same, entry id shall not change. Why cannot you just create new uploads in the new oasis using the old raw file archive?
proposed_answer: It think in my references there is the upload_id and the entry_id, so if my uploads get a new upload_id the reference is broken and cannot be found anymore.

question: A quick question about the Simmetry class that Area C develops in nomad-simulation plugin: do you store some crystal information there or somewhere else? If yes, do you have a fixed way to store HKL indices that is not in a string? I received some comments from a user about the ambiguous nature of a string for that. One can indeed write "001", "[001]", "0 0 1", etc. We could think to a standard way of dealing with that. Either a special quantity or a subsection with three quantities inside. @Micha any solution in your custom Oasis for this?
proposed_answer: no not at all

title: general-comp
question: So `Symmetry` is a sub-section of `ModelSystem(System)` (System is defined in base sections). We store subentity positions under another sub-section called `Cell` (which is inheriting from an abstract concept, `GeometricSpace`). If these "subentities" are atoms, we use `AtomicCell(Cell)` instead and store the atoms positions and their states (orbitals, oxidation, magnetic moments) info in a sub-section of AtomicCell called `AtomsState`. Those are the main ingredients of our system section. Regarding orientation, I am not the best to answer this. Our data is mainly single grains, but @Joseph Ru …
proposed_answer: yes it does. I believe this quantity should be grouped somewhere in a class of crystalline surfaces properties. But if you don't deal directly with this info, we can figure out where to place it. I think in some subsection of Cell class, from your structure. Thanks, I will let you know

title: Oasis to Central
question: So, for instance, if I publish something on my Oasis with a bespoke parser, only the logs of the entry will be pushed on the central NOMAD and so, the entry will be visible there but only the `LOGS` will display information, is that it ? And what about the same thing but with already handled parsers, let's say like the VASP one ? In this case, will only the logs be pushed or the rest of the entry will follow as the central NOMAD already know VASP ?
proposed_answer: Currently nothing will be pushed Not even the logs Unless you opt in. And even of you do I'm not sure if the log transfer is running. Anyways, that's the "backend" logs so to say. The push of entries is something we are currently working on but like I said, nothing is currently being transferred. Maybe @Markus Scheidgen knows more about the latest status?

question: Thanks Lauri. The only problem in the documentation I found, is that the quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:16.1.1 image is not configured to use a H2 database by default, but looks for a MySQL DB, and therefore fails at startup. It was necessary to set DB_*** environment variables to connect to a DB successfully, at a minimum DB_VENDOR=h2. Perhaps a documentation update might be worth considering?
proposed_answer: Started a thread.

id: NOMAD - area-b-devs - @Lukas Pielsticker fyi: Whitespaces are [1230074365442392135] (2024-04-01 to 2024-06-30)|q11-a12

title: @Lukas Pielsticker fyi: Whitespaces are
question: The problem is more complicated than I thought... my solution was to simply compare the paths whenever I encounter a variadic name, but what I forgot is that we have to do it recursively when we this group has yet another group which has been renamed. This is the case when we rename the entry. With the current validation this appears to be very complex. So I'm thinking of the alternative way of directly validating the path against the xml files without comparing it to the generic dict we already have. I think this will be much simpler when we have a greater variation of names. @Sandor Brockhau …
proposed_answer: You mean why are we currently using an empty Template to validate against instead of using the XML files directly based on the filled template?

id: NOMAD - area-b-devs - @Lukas Pielsticker fyi: Whitespaces are [1230074365442392135] (2024-04-01 to 2024-06-30)|q14-a15
thread_id: NOMAD - area-b-devs - @Lukas Pielsticker fyi: Whitespaces are [1230074365442392135] (2024-04-01 to 2024-06-30)
title: @Lukas Pielsticker fyi: Whitespaces are
question: We use an empty and generic named one to validate the filled template against. You're asking why we use that instead of directly checking concepts from the XML to verify the filled template, right?
proposed_answer: Yes

id: NOMAD - area-b-devs - @Lukas Pielsticker fyi: Whitespaces are [1230074365442392135] (2024-04-01 to 2024-06-30)|q16-a17
thread_id: NOMAD - area-b-devs - @Lukas Pielsticker fyi: Whitespaces are [1230074365442392135] (2024-04-01 to 2024-06-30)
title: @Lukas Pielsticker fyi: Whitespaces are
question: The reason to do that are two fold. The major reason is to make sure the reader is simply not deleting a key in the Template or returning a new empty dict and we are left with only keys provided. But not necessarily the keys asked for by the XML. Or if the keys are manipulated and the concepts are not named right. Then the validation wouldn't work. Maybe we can meet and discuss this quickly?
proposed_answer: Sure, scrum?

question: Hi all, I am curious how others feel about ordering of author lists. It depends of course on what the contribution is about, but for very internal and area-specific FAIRmat developments, I would intuitively think something like: presenter, domain experts/direct contributors in order of contribution amt, PIs/Collaborators in alphabetical order, Area Leader, Area Coordinator Other suggestions?
proposed_answer: Let me also share how this is currently done in Area F. Main author (presenter), FAIRmat members contributing directly, Area coordinator, Task leaders (PIs)*, Area leader, I include Claudia as well because she is usually very involved in Area F activities. *Involvement of task leaders is based whether they have effectively contributed to the work by discussion, mentoring, or providing guidance to the activity OR providing feedback on the abstract after it is prepared and shared with them.

title: general-comp
question: Just relaying some user feedback (sent to me by e-mail): they're asking for the capability of *renaming (editing) folders without having to re-upload* them. I'm not sure if we want to allow write-access to the original files and whether we want to edit compressed folders... Alternatively maybe an annotation files that also gets read by the GUI to modify naming (but then you can't restructure folders)?
proposed_answer: I don't see it as a bad thing. We just have to educate users on how the processing works, and they can even improve their tree structure by editing and moving files around directly in NOMAD. Furthermore, I think the whole Upload page should be re-structure around "what the user uploads", meaning, what is the folder structure, and not around "what are the entries generated", which is less intuitive and a bit cumbersome when handling thousands of data points

title: area-c-fairmat-internal
question: You would classify these atomic descriptors as analysis? IMO it's a matter of perspective I guess, but the question is if these descriptors should be calculated automatically for all entries. I am wondering if it is possible to only calculate/store them at the time of request? I disagree with his assessment of the SOAP vector parameters: "The current schema for SOAP descriptors (shown below) includes entries for the parameters that were used to generate the descriptors e.g. r_cut, n_max etc. These will be the same for all structures in the database so this seems inefficient? My proposal is to …
proposed_answer: What I meant: these descriptors, sames as with DOSFingerprints or XRDAnalysis, are analysis tools that are calculated on the fly by NOMAD. I would somehow make a bit of a distinction of what they are from the normalizers. Btw, the difference between Areas A, B analysis notebooks and our implementations on SOAP and DOSFingerprints is that, for now, our side is automatically integrated in NOMAD. But both cases are equivalent. That's why is a bit confusing for me: again, I thought that "normalization" was about interoperability, not about generating new data

title: moderator-only
question: @moderator Just a note: There are channels marked private (lock symbol) that we as moderators can view by default. Please respect the privacy of these channels. It is possible to create completely private channels that only invited members (i.e., not moderators) can view. @Sebastian Brückner Could you share how you did this?
proposed_answer: I did this via creating a group chat with the people I have a as friends. So it happens actually outside the NOMAD discord server but on the general discord server.

title: Error while running the Tutorial
question: When running a north tool for the first time, the docker image will be downloaded. This takes a few minutes, and you might get a 504 timeout error. But if you retry after a couple minutes, the tool should start out fine, like happened here. @Adam Fekete: is there any way to pre-download the images during the distribution bootup? That would be nice, at least for the most used tools like jupyter.
proposed_answer: could you point me the script of the deployment? in short: if this is a k8s deployment we should have the tools already pre-pulled.
